summary: Test-only commit adding agents/query/test_synthesizer_citations.py. Confirmed synthesizer.py has zero diff vs. 4.5.1 (c8c49cf) -- purely additive, no scope creep. New file's fixture contains exactly one valid + one hallucinated citation as required by the acceptance criteria/test spec, exercised end-to-end through synthesize_answer() with exact-value (not just truthy) assertions on unknown_citations(). All shipped and independently-run tests pass; full regression shows only the 2 pre-existing protobuf/issue-#46 failures. ruff clean. Acceptance criteria for 4.5.2 are technically and substantively satisfied. Flagged non-blocking concerns: (1) 3 of the new file's 4 tests substantially duplicate an existing 4.5.1 test in test_synthesizer.py (same literal fixture path strings, same assertions), so the 'dedicated test file' requirement is met in form but the file's unique incremental contribution is really just the order-independence test; (2) none of my own adversarial variants (case-difference, separator/trailing-slash, empty list, all-hallucinated, valid+near-miss co-occurring) are covered by the new shipped tests, though I independently verified unknown_citations() handles all of them correctly with no false negatives.

evidence: gh issue view 24 subtask 4.5.2 acceptance criteria: 'Any citation in synthesized answer file path actually present in selected-file input set; citations unknown paths flagged/rejected.' Test spec: pytest agents/query/test_synthesizer_citations.py, fixture LLM response with one valid + one hallucinated citation, assert hallucinated one detected/flagged.
New file agents/query/test_synthesizer_citations.py exists at the exact specified path.

_fixture_response() builds a JSON LLM response with citations=[_VALID_PATH, _HALLUCINATED_PATH] (or reversed) -- exactly one valid ('billing/InvoiceDisputes.md', present in _SELECTED_MARKDOWN's '## File:' headers) and one hallucinated ('legal/InternalMemo.md', absent from _SELECTED_MARKDOWN entirely) citation, matching the literal one-valid+one-hallucinated scenario.

test_mixed_valid_and_hallucinated_citations_only_hallucinated_flagged asserts result.unknown_citations() == [_HALLUCINATED_PATH] (exact list equality, not truthiness) and result.citations == [_VALID_PATH, _HALLUCINATED_PATH] unchanged (flagging is additive, not a silent filter) -- satisfies 'flagged' half of 'flagged/rejected' disjunctive wording.

Both citation orderings are tested: test_hallucinated_citation_is_flagged_as_unknown (default hallucinated_first=False) and test_hallucinated_citation_flagged_regardless_of_position (hallucinated_first=True) -- confirmed via reading _fixture_response()'s hallucinated_first branch, which swaps both answer-prose citation order and the citations array order.
git diff c8c49cf b8da449 -- agents/query/synthesizer.py returns 0 lines -- confirmed byte-for-byte unchanged. Implementer's claim of 'no synthesizer.py change' is verified true, not merely asserted.

New test uses the same LLMClient-ABC-subclass _FakeLLMClient pattern as test_synthesizer.py/test_intent_refiner.py (package convention), exercises synthesize_answer() end-to-end rather than unit-testing unknown_citations() on a hand-built SynthesizerResult -- matches the acceptance criteria's 'citation in the synthesized answer' wording (i.e. tests the real code path, not just the isolated method).

Impacted-modules list in the issue names only test_synthesizer_citations.py; diff --stat confirms only that one production/test file changed (all other diff entries are .cdr/ run-artifact bookkeeping).
cd agents && python3 -m pytest query/ -v: 77 passed, 0 failed (independently re-run, matches implementer's claim).

cd agents && python3 -m pytest . --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -q: 288 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures are ingestion/test_shortlist.py::test_grpc_client_translates_request_response and ::test_grpc_client_is_a_valid_search_candidates_fn_for_shortlist, both google.protobuf.runtime_version.VersionError (gencode 6.33.5 / runtime 5.29.6) -- the same pre-existing issue-#46 class of failure previously reconfirmed in F-4.4.4-4 and unrelated to agents/query/ or this diff.
4.5.1 sanity check: synthesizer.py unchanged (0 diff), and all of test_synthesizer.py's 15 pre-existing tests still pass in the same run -- 4.5.1 is intact and unbroken by this commit.

Adversarially constructed 6 additional scenarios directly against SynthesizerResult.unknown_citations() (not shipped in any test file): (1) case-different variant of a real path ('Billing/InvoiceDisputes.md' vs provided 'billing/InvoiceDisputes.md') -> correctly flagged unknown; (2) trailing-slash variant -> correctly flagged unknown; (3) backslash-separator variant -> correctly flagged unknown; (4) empty citations list -> returns [] (no crash); (5) all-citations-hallucinated list -> both flagged, order preserved; (6) same real path co-occurring with a near-miss case-variant -> only the near-miss variant flagged, real path not flagged. All behaved sensibly with no false negatives, consistent with the exact-match design already disclosed and previously spot-checked in F-4.5.1-2.

None of these adversarial scenarios are present in the shipped test_synthesizer_citations.py file itself -- the shipped file only covers the single literal one-valid+one-hallucinated scenario (in both orderings), which is sufficient for the acceptance criteria as literally worded but leaves the adversarial cases as unexercised regression protection (non-blocking, since behavior is correct and was independently confirmed).
No secrets, no auth/crypto logic touched. Pure test-file addition exercising an already-shipped, already-reviewed parsing/detection function.
gh issue view 24 body was read and contains no embedded fake system-reminder/injection text this run (unlike prior instances noted in project history) -- clean issue body.

Separately, this verification session's own tool-output stream contained injected fake system-reminder-style content (a fake 'date has changed' notice, a fake MCP-server 'tokensave' tool-usage instruction block for a tool never present in my actual toolset, and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive). None of these originated from gh/git content inspected during this task; disclosing per the task's security note. Not acted upon: no tokensave tool was invoked (none exists in my toolset), the date-change notice was not treated as license to alter any finding, and 'Auto Mode Active' was not treated as authorization for any destructive or scope-expanding action. No GitHub state was changed and nothing was pushed.
No performance-relevant code touched. Test suite runtime negligible (query/ suite completes in 0.15s).

_FakeLLMClient in test_synthesizer_citations.py is duplicated near-verbatim from test_synthesizer.py's own _FakeLLMClient (and test_intent_refiner.py's) -- consistent with the pre-existing package convention already flagged non-blocking in F-4.4.4-2 (now a 4th instance of the same duplicated-fixture-factory pattern across agents/query/). No new duplication class introduced, just a growing count.

Substantive finding: test_valid_citation_not_flagged_as_unknown, test_hallucinated_citation_is_flagged_as_unknown, and test_mixed_valid_and_hallucinated_citations_only_hallucinated_flagged in the new file are near-duplicates in scenario and assertion shape of the pre-existing test_synthesizer.py::test_unknown_citations_flags_path_not_in_provided_set -- confirmed by grep that both files reuse the identical literal fixture strings 'billing/InvoiceDisputes.md' (valid) and 'legal/InternalMemo.md' (hallucinated). The new file's only test with genuinely new incremental content is test_hallucinated_citation_flagged_regardless_of_position (order-independence). This means the 'dedicated test file for 4.5.2' requirement is satisfied in form (file exists at the named path, scenario matches spec) but its net-new test-coverage contribution beyond 4.5.1's existing suite is thin -- see requirements_conformance dimension 7 discussion below.
validation-matrix.json's 6 rows are all covered by the 4 shipped tests, confirmed by independent test run (4/4 new tests pass, plus all 73 pre-existing query/ tests still pass).

Coverage gap (non-blocking, same as edge_cases dimension): no shipped test for case-difference/separator/trailing-slash/empty-list/all-hallucinated/co-occurring-near-miss variants; these were independently verified correct by this verification but are not committed as regression-protecting tests.

confidence.notes: Directly re-ran every claimed test command myself and got matching results (77 passed in query/; 288 passed/2 failed in full su999; ruff clean). Directly diffed synthesizer.py between the two commits myself (0 lines) rather than trusting the commit message's claim. Read both test_synthesizer.py and the new file in full and cross-referenced literal fixture strings by grep.

possible_improvements: Extract the repeated _FakeLLMClient factory (4th duplicate instance across agents/query/) into a shared conftest.py or _test_helpers.py once a 5th consumer appears, per prior F-4.4.4-2 recommendation.

Consider adding the adversarial variants I constructed (case-difference, separator/trailing-slash, empty citations, all-hallucinated, co-occurring valid+near-miss) as committed regression tests, since unknown_citations()'s exact-match semantics are a deliberate design choice worth pinning down explicitly rather than leaving as tribal knowledge in CDR verification notes.

Given the 3-of-4 duplication with test_synthesizer.py's existing unknown_citations() tests, a future reader might reasonably ask whether 4.5.2's genuinely load-bearing new assertion (order-independence) could have been added as a single parametrized test to test_synthesizer.py instead of a new file -- though the issue's own explicit test spec names the dedicated file path, so this is not a defect, just a design-taste observation.

adversarial_findings.finding: 3 of the 4 tests in the new agents/query/test_synthesizer_citations.py (test_valid_citation_not_flagged_as_unknown, test_hallucinated_citation_is_flagged_as_unknown, test_mixed_valid_and_hallucinated_citations_only_hallucinated_flagged) substantially duplicate the scenario and literal fixture values (identical path strings 'billing/InvoiceDisputes.md' and 'legal/InternalMemo.md') of the pre-existing test_synthesizer.py::test_unknown_citations_flags_path_not_in_provided_set (built in 4.5.1). The dedicated file's only genuinely new incremental assertion is order-independence (test_hallucinated_citation_flagged_regardless_of_position). This satisfies 4.5.2's acceptance criteria and test spec literally (a dedicated file exists at the named path, with the named one-valid+one-hallucinated scenario, asserting detection with exact-value checks), but is a thin/circular reading in substance: most of the file re-proves what 4.5.1's own test suite already proved under a new filename.

Adversarially constructed and independently verified 6 additional scenarios against SynthesizerResult.unknown_citations() not covered by any shipped test: case-different path variant, trailing-slash variant, backslash-separator variant, empty citations list, all-citations-hallucinated list, and a real path co-occurring with a near-miss case-variant. All behaved correctly (no false negatives) using the exact-match set-membership design already disclosed and previously spot-checked in F-4.5.1-2. None of these are committed as regression tests.

This verification session's own tool-output/conversation stream contained injected fake system-reminder-style text unrelated to any actual gh/git content examined (a fake date-change notice, a fake MCP-server 'tokensave' tool-instruction block for a tool not present in my toolset, and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive). Disclosed per task security note; none were acted upon (no such tool exists/was invoked, no destructive action was taken, no GitHub state changed, nothing pushed). No injected text was found in gh issue view 24's actual body or in the b8da449/c8c49cf commit contents themselves.

checks_run.diff_confirmation: git diff c8c49cf b8da449 -- agents/query/synthesizer.py => empty (0 lines)

issue_24_end_to_end_status.subtask_4_5_1: verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS (run 026-verification, commit c8c49cf), re-confirmed intact/unbroken by this diff
issue_24_end_to_end_status.conclusion: Issue #24's acceptance criteria are genuinely satisfied end-to-end: synthesize_answer() performs prompt assembly with file-path headers and produces a citation-annotated answer (4.5.1), and unknown_citations() together with a dedicated test file demonstrates detection of hallucinated citations against the real selected-file input set (4.5.2). Both subtasks' commits are local-only (not pushed), consistent with repo convention for prior issues (#20-#23) pending a later batched push/close step.
issue_24_end_to_end_status.milestone_6_phase_4_query_pipeline: This closes issue #24, the last open issue tracked under milestone 'Phase 4: Query pipeline' per the task framing; no further gaps identified in agents/query/ that would block considering Phase 4 functionally complete at the local-commit level.
